title: GitHub Profile, Repository Health, and Verifiable Work · Opportunity Brief: Repository Entry
summary: A profile has a dense contribution graph and many forked tutorials, but reviewers cannot find three relevant, runnable, maintained, or honestly attributed examples. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the repository entry increment in selective GitHub evidence route. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.
Write a concise profile route that helps a reviewer reach role, proof, boundaries, contact choice, and accessible alternatives.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Repository Entry · Theory Repository Entry
steps.instruction:
theory case — A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Repository Entry contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Repository Entry · Theory Repository Entry.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence. For Predict Repository Entry · Theory Repository Entry, keep Repository Entry connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The worked example variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence. A defensible solution applies Repository Entry, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to selective GitHub evidence route.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-4
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A screenshot and technology badge explain a project sufficiently. This fails the Repository Entry contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: O*NET Software Developers Occupation Report (O*NET current occupation data reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls task, work-activity, skill, knowledge, work-style, and technology evidence used to decompose roles rather than chase job-title labels.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.
